perturbation.original_text: TERM: This Agreement pertains only to the Confidential Information that is disclosed between the Effective Date (last signature date) and one year from that date. Each Party receiving Confidential Information under this Agreement shall maintain the information in confidence in accordance with the terms of this Agreement notwithstanding any termination of this Agreement for a period of three years from the date the Confidential Information is disclosed to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: TERM: This Agreement pertains only to the Confidential Information that is disclosed between the Effective Date (last signature date) and one year from that date. Each Party receiving Confidential Data under this Agreement shall maintain the information in confidence in accordance with the terms of this Agreement notwithstanding any termination of this Agreement for a period of three years from the date the Confidential Information is disclosed to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' creates ambiguity. Are they the same? The section then reverts back to 'Confidential Information', causing confusion about the obligations and scope of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERM: This Agreement pertains only to the Confidential Information that is disclosed between the Effective Date (last signature date) and one year from that date. Each Party receiving Confidential Data under this Agreement shall maintain the information in confidence in accordance with the terms of this Agreement notwithstanding any termination of this Agreement for a period of three years from the date the Confidential Information is disclosed to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in the Changed Text, while the Contradicted Text uses both terms, creates a Misaligned Terminology contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation states that this change introduces ambiguity about whether 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data' are the same, causing confusion about the obligations and scope of the agreement. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology, leading to an in-text contradiction because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: IDENTIFICATION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: All Confidential Information shall be identified prior to disclosure with an appropriate marking or identification such as CONFIDENTIAL or any similar legend. If such information is disclosed either orally or visually, then the Parties will use reasonable efforts to assure protection pursuant to this Agreement. The Parties shall use reasonable effort to reduce such oral or visual Confidential Information to tangible form otherwise in compliance with this Agreement and furnish a copy to the Parties within thirty (30) business days of the original oral or visual disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: IDENTIFICATION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: All Sensitive Information shall be identified prior to disclosure with an appropriate marking or identification such as CONFIDENTIAL or any similar legend. If such information is disclosed either orally or visually, then the Parties will use reasonable efforts to assure protection pursuant to this Agreement. The Parties shall use reasonable effort to reduce such oral or visual Confidential Information to tangible form otherwise in compliance with this Agreement and furnish a copy to the Parties within thirty (30) business days of the original oral or visual disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The text changes 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' at the beginning of the section. However, the rest of the paragraph continues to refer to 'Confidential Information,' and therefore leads to ambiguity, particularly in determining the scope of what needs to be marked as confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IDENTIFICATION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: All Sensitive Information shall be identified prior to disclosure with an appropriate marking or identification such as CONFIDENTIAL or any similar legend. If such information is disclosed either orally or visually, then the Parties will use reasonable efforts to assure protection pursuant to this Agreement. The Parties shall use reasonable effort to reduce such oral or visual Confidential Information to tangible form otherwise in compliance with this Agreement and furnish a copy to the Parties within thirty (30) business days of the original oral or visual disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' at the beginning of the section. However, the rest of the paragraph continues to refer to 'Confidential Information.' The Contradicted Text contains this same modification. This discrepancy leads to ambiguity, particularly in determining the scope of what needs to be marked as confidential. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. This creates a strong contradiction as it is unclear whether the clause applies to Sensitive Information, Confidential Information, or both.

perturbation.original_text: NON-DISCLOSURE: The Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party individual, corporation, or other entity without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party and shall further limit the circulation and disclosure of Confidential Information within its own organization to its employees having a "need to know" the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth in this Agreement, and to ensure that such employees are informed of the confidential nature thereof and agree to and are required to observe the provision of confidentiality set forth herein. The Receiving Party will not attempt to determine the content or structure, or otherwise reverse engineer or decompile any material sample, hardware or software to which it is provided access pursuant to this Agreement except as expressly provided under Section 3 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: NON-DISCLOSURE: The Receiving Party shall not disclose Protected Information to any third party individual, corporation, or other entity without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party and shall further limit the circulation and disclosure of Confidential Information within its own organization to its employees having a "need to know" the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth in this Agreement, and to ensure that such employees are informed of the confidential nature thereof and agree to and are required to observe the provision of confidentiality set forth herein. The Receiving Party will not attempt to determine the content or structure, or otherwise reverse engineer or decompile any material sample, hardware or software to which it is provided access pursuant to this Agreement except as expressly provided under Section 3 of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: In the non-disclosure clause, changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' at the start of the section introduces a discrepancy. The section then continues to use 'Confidential Information,' creating ambiguity. Does 'Protected Information' encompass all 'Confidential Information,' or only a subset? The contradiction arises because the prohibition seemingly applies to something different than what the rest of the section defines as needing protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE: The Receiving Party shall not disclose Protected Information to any third party individual, corporation, or other entity without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party and shall further limit the circulation and disclosure of Confidential Information within its own organization to its employees having a "need to know" the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth in this Agreement, and to ensure that such employees are informed of the confidential nature thereof and agree to and are required to observe the provision of confidentiality set forth herein. The Receiving Party will not attempt to determine the content or structure, or otherwise reverse engineer or decompile any material sample, hardware or software to which it is provided access pursuant to this Agreement except as expressly provided under Section 3 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' at the beginning of the non-disclosure section, while the rest of the section continues to use 'Confidential Information.' This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether 'Protected Information' is a subset of, equivalent to, or different from 'Confidential Information,' making the scope of the non-disclosure obligation ambiguous. The contradicted text is the same clause where the change was made. According to the perturbation definition, this is a clear instance of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the contract uses multiple terms without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: EXCEPTIONS TO CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: No restriction shall exist under this Agreement with respect to any portion of the Confidential Information that is: a. established by the Receiving Party to have been known by it at the time of receipt and reduced to written form. b. published or otherwise becomes generally known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party. c. received from a third party without similar restrictions and without breach of the restrictions within this Agreement. d. independently developed by the Receiving Party prior to receipt of the Confidential Information. e. furnished to a third party by the Disclosing Party without similar restriction on the recipients' rights. f. approved in writing for release by the Disclosing Party. g. required by court order or government agency to be disclosed. h. disclosed by inspection of a product incorporating the Confidential Information after the product has been disclosed or sold.
perturbation.changed_text: EXCEPTIONS TO COVERED INFORMATION: No restriction shall exist under this Agreement with respect to any portion of the Confidential Information that is: a. established by the Receiving Party to have been known by it at the time of receipt and reduced to written form. b. published or otherwise becomes generally known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party. c. received from a third party without similar restrictions and without breach of the restrictions within this Agreement. d. independently developed by the Receiving Party prior to receipt of the Confidential Information. e. furnished to a third party by the Disclosing Party without similar restriction on the recipients' rights. f. approved in writing for release by the Disclosing Party. g. required by court order or government agency to be disclosed. h. disclosed by inspection of a product incorporating the Confidential Information after the product has been disclosed or sold.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'EXCEPTIONS TO CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' to 'EXCEPTIONS TO COVERED INFORMATION:' makes the scope of exceptions ambiguous. While the exceptions listed still refer to 'Confidential Information,' the introductory phrase suggests a broader category of information might be excluded. The contradiction is whether the exceptions only apply to 'Confidential Information' as specifically defined, or to a wider set of 'COVERED INFORMATION' that remains undefined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXCEPTIONS TO COVERED INFORMATION: No restriction shall exist under this Agreement with respect to any portion of the Confidential Information that is: a. established by the Receiving Party to have been known by it at the time of receipt and reduced to written form. b. published or otherwise becomes generally known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party. c. received from a third party without similar restrictions and without breach of the restrictions within this Agreement. d. independently developed by the Receiving Party prior to receipt of the Confidential Information. e. furnished to a third party by the Disclosing Party without similar restriction on the recipients' rights. f. approved in writing for release by the Disclosing Party. g. required by court order or government agency to be disclosed. h. disclosed by inspection of a product incorporating the Confidential Information after the product has been disclosed or sold.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' with 'COVERED INFORMATION' in the title. The listed exceptions in the changed text, however, still explicitly refer to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because the title suggests exceptions apply to a broader category ('COVERED INFORMATION'), while the content refers only to 'Confidential Information.' The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning the contradiction exists within the same section. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as 'COVERED INFORMATION' is not defined and creates ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: STANDARD OF CARE: The Receiving Party shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: STANDARD OF CARE: The Receiving Party shall protect the disclosed Sensitive Data by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Confidential Information' is changed to 'Sensitive Data,' but then the rest of the sentence refers back to 'Confidential Information'. This creates uncertainty about the standard of care. Is the receiving party required to protect the disclosed 'Sensitive Data' with the same care as their own 'Confidential Information'? What if their own sensitive data isn't treated as 'Confidential Information' internally? This inconsistency directly affects the enforceability of the standard of care provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STANDARD OF CARE: The Receiving Party shall protect the disclosed Sensitive Data by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Data' in the STANDARD OF CARE clause, while the contradicted text (which is the same clause) still refers to both 'Sensitive Data' and 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because the receiving party is required to protect 'Sensitive Data' with the same care as their own 'Confidential Information', leading to uncertainty and inconsistency, as it is unclear if their sensitive data is treated as 'Confidential Information' internally, thus creating a conflict, according to the Misaligned Terminology definition.
